title: When the sampling rate is 0, all data will be returned.
description: ### Company or project name _No response_ ### Describe what's wrong When the sampling rate is 0, all data will be returned. Schema of Table ```sql CREATE TABLE genai_eva_trace.otel_traces_main_for_sampling ( `trace_id` String, `feature` String, `account_id` String, `extension_id` String, `start_timestamp` DateTime64(9) ) ENGINE = MergeTree PARTITION BY toDate(start_timestamp) ORDER BY (feature, account_id, extension_id, start_timestamp, cityHash64(trace_id)) SAMPLE BY cityHash64(trace_id) TTL toDate(start_timestamp) + toIntervalDay(7) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192; ``` ### Does it reproduce on the most recent release? Yes ### How to reproduce version: v25.1.7.20 ### Expected behavior _No response_ ### Error message and/or stacktrace _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE genai_eva_trace.otel_traces_main_for_sampling_extended (trace_id VARCHAR, feature VARCHAR, account_id VARCHAR, extension_id VARCHAR, start_timestamp TIMESTAMP, status VARCHAR);

INSERT INTO genai_eva_trace.otel_traces_main_for_sampling_extended (trace_id, feature, account_id, extension_id, start_timestamp, status) VALUES ('1', 'featureA', 'account1', 'ext1', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, NULL);

INSERT INTO genai_eva_trace.otel_traces_main_for_sampling_extended (trace_id, feature, account_id, extension_id, start_timestamp, status) VALUES ('2', 'featureB', 'account2', 'ext2', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, 'active');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM genai_eva_trace.otel_traces_main_for_sampling_extended WHERE status IS NULL;
key_bug_features: Sampling rate set to 0 returns all data
